Module com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java
Package com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java

Interface ArrayVerifier<A,E>

Type Parameters:
A - the type of the array
E - the type of elements in the array
All Superinterfaces:
ExtensibleObjectVerifier<ArrayVerifier<A,E>,A>
All Known Implementing Classes:
ArrayVerifierImpl
public interface ArrayVerifier<A,E> extends ExtensibleObjectVerifier<ArrayVerifier<A,E>,A>
Verifies the requirements of an array.

All methods (except those found in ExtensibleObjectVerifier) imply ExtensibleObjectVerifier.isNotNull().

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    CollectionVerifier<Collection<E>,E>
    asCollection()
    Returns a verifier for the actual value as a collection.

    Ensures that the actual value contains an element.

    Ensures that the actual value contains an element.

    Ensures that the actual value contains all of the specified elements.

    Ensures that the actual value contains all of the specified elements.

    Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.

    Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>

    Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>

    Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.

    Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>

    Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    doesNotContainAll(Collection<E> elements)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain all of the specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    doesNotContainAll(Collection<E> elements, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain all of specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>

    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    doesNotContainAny(Collection<E> elements, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    doesNotContainDuplicates()
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain any duplicate elements.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    doesNotContainExactly(Collection<E> other)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
    ArrayVerifier<A,E>
    doesNotContainExactly(Collection<E> other, String name)
    Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.

    Methods declared in interface com.github.cowwoc.requirements.java.extension.ExtensibleObjectVerifier

    asString, asString, getActual, isEqualTo, isEqualTo, isInstanceOf, isNotEqualTo, isNotEqualTo, isNotInstanceOf, isNotNull, isNotOneOf, isNotSameObjectAs, isNull, isOneOf, isSameObjectAs

  • Method Details

    • isEmpty

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> isEmpty()
      Ensures that the actual value is empty.
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array is not empty

    • isNotEmpty

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> isNotEmpty()
      Ensures that the actual value is not empty.
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array is empty

    • contains

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> contains(E expected)
      Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
      Parameters:
      expected - the element
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain expected

    • contains

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> contains(E expected, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value contains an element.
      Parameters:
      expected - the element
      name - the name of the element
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain expected. If name is blank.

    • containsExactly

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> containsExactly(Collection<E> expected)
      Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array is missing any element found in expected; if the array contains any element not found in expected

    • containsExactly

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> containsExactly(Collection<E> expected, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value contains the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      name - the name of the elements
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array is missing any element found in expected. If the array contains any element not found in expected. If name is blank.

    • containsAny

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> containsAny(Collection<E> expected)
      Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain any of expected

    • containsAny

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> containsAny(Collection<E> expected, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value contains any of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      name - the name of the elements
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain any of expected. If name is blank.

    • containsAll

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> containsAll(Collection<E> expected)
      Ensures that the actual value contains all of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain all of expected

    • containsAll

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> containsAll(Collection<E> expected, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value contains all of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      expected - the elements that must exist
      name - the name of the elements
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if expected or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array does not contain all of expected. If name is blank.

    • doesNotContain

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> doesNotContain(E element)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
      Parameters:
      element - the element that must not exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains element

    • doesNotContain

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> doesNotContain(E element, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain an element.
      Parameters:
      element - the element that must not exist
      name - the name of the element
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if name is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains element. If name is blank.

    • doesNotContainExactly

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> doesNotContainExactly(Collection<E> other)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
      Parameters:
      other - the elements that must not exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if other is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains all of the elements in other; nothing less, nothing more.

    • doesNotContainExactly

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> doesNotContainExactly(Collection<E> other, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain exactly the specified elements; nothing less, nothing more.
      Parameters:
      other - the elements that must not exist
      name - the name of the collection
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if other or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains all of the elements in other; nothing less, nothing more. If name is blank.

    • doesNotContainAny

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> doesNotContainAny(Collection<E> elements)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements that must not exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if elements is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains any of elements

    • doesNotContainAny

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> doesNotContainAny(Collection<E> elements, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain any of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements that must not exist
      name - the name of the elements
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if elements or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains any of elements. If name is blank.

    • doesNotContainAll

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> doesNotContainAll(Collection<E> elements)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain all of the specified elements.
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements that must not exist
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if elements is null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains all of elements

    • doesNotContainAll

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> doesNotContainAll(Collection<E> elements, String name)
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain all of specified elements.
      Parameters:
      elements - the elements that must not exist
      name - the name of the elements
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if elements or name are null
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains all of elements. If name is blank.

    • doesNotContainDuplicates

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> doesNotContainDuplicates()
      Ensures that the actual value does not contain any duplicate elements.
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the array contains any duplicate elements

    • isSorted

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> isSorted(Comparator<E> comparator)
      Ensures that the actual value is sorted.
      Parameters:
      comparator - the comparator that defines the order of the elements
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the collection is not sorted
      See Also:
      • Comparator.naturalOrder()

    • length

      SizeVerifier length()
      Returns a verifier over the array's length.
      Returns:
      a verifier over the array's length

    • length

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> length(Consumer<SizeVerifier> consumer)
      Verifies nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.
      Parameters:
      consumer - verifies the array's length
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null

    • asCollection

      CollectionVerifier<Collection<E>,E> asCollection()
      Returns a verifier for the actual value as a collection.
      Returns:
      a verifier for the actual value as a collection

    • asCollection

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> asCollection(Consumer<CollectionVerifier<Collection<E>,E>> consumer)
      Verifies nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.
      Parameters:
      consumer - verifies the actual value as a collection
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
      NullPointerException - if consumer is null

    • asList

      ListVerifier<List<E>,E> asList()
      Returns a verifier for the actual value as a list.
      Returns:
      a verifier for the actual value as a list

    • asList

      ArrayVerifier<A,E> asList(Consumer<ListVerifier<List<E>,E>> consumer)
      Verifies nested requirements. This mechanism can be used to group related requirements.
      Parameters:
      consumer - verifies the actual value as a list
      Returns:
      the updated verifier
      Throws:
